pages.raw_text:
(By United Press Leased Wire)


MINNEAPOLIS, March e.-Beset
before and behind by snowslides
which threatened almost momen-
tarily to carry il to destruction,
stopped by washouts, delayed and
forced to retreat by wrecks ahead
and at one time within few miles
OF the great avalanche which crush
ed two trains at Wellington, WASH,
what is officially known as train
No. 2 of the Great Northern, leav-
ing Seattle February 26, arrived in
Minneapolis last night, the first
train through from the coast in four
days. II train ever bore a charm
ed life II was this same No. 2. That
is what every passenger says.


No. 2 left Seattle q week ago
Saturday afternoon. Perhaps it
was an impromptu prayer meeting
held on the train last Sunday night
by Jealous evangelist which gave
the train its freedom from real dis
aster, for onlya few hours later No.
